title: Asset
description: A non-property asset: financial accounts, personal property, vehicles, digital assets, business interests, Islamic finance instruments, etc.

id.description: Unique identifier for this asset within the INHERIT document.

name.description: Display name for this asset. Should be specific enough to identify the item during probate.

category.$comment: Top-level categories from the INHERIT Product Taxonomy (derived from the Standard Product Taxonomy, MIT licence). financial: bank accounts, savings, investments, pensions, shares, bonds, cryptocurrency, insurance. property_contents: furniture, kitchenware, electronics, tools, garden and outdoor items. vehicle: cars, motorcycles, boats, caravans, classic vehicles. jewellery_watches: jewellery, watches, precious stones, costume jewellery. art: paintings, sculptures, prints, photography, mixed media. antiques: antique furniture, ceramics, silverware, clocks. collectibles: stamps, coins, model railways, vinyl records, trading cards, memorabilia. musical_instruments: pianos, guitars, orchestral instruments, vintage instruments. books_manuscripts: rare books, first editions, manuscripts, maps. wine_spirits: wine collections, whisky, spirits, cellar contents. clothing_textiles: designer clothing, vintage fashion, textiles, rugs. firearms_sporting: firearms (require licensing), sporting equipment, fishing tackle. digital: domain names, social media accounts, subscriptions, digital media, crypto wallets. business: business interests, partnerships, intellectual property, stock compensation. islamic_financial: sukuk, takaful, Islamic deposits — internationally recognized financial instruments defined by AAOIFI standards and regulated by secular financial authorities. other: any asset not covered above. Use the subcategory field for finer classification — valid subcategories per category are listed in reference-data/category-guidance.json.
category.description: Top-level category from the INHERIT Product Taxonomy. Use the `subcategory` field for finer classification. See `reference-data/category-guidance.json` for valid subcategories, recommended identifiers, and photography guidance per category.

subcategory.description: Finer classification within the top-level category. Values are defined in reference-data/category-guidance.json but the field is freeform to accommodate jurisdiction-specific asset types. Examples: financial → 'pension', collectibles → 'model_railways', vehicle → 'classic_vehicle'.

estimatedValue.description: Owner's estimate of the asset's current value, in minor currency units.

professionalValuation.description: Professional valuation of the asset, in minor currency units.

valuationDate.description: Date of the most recent valuation.

valuationConfidence.$comment: estimated: owner's own estimate. professional: assessed by a qualified valuer. official: valuation agreed with tax authority for probate, inheritance tax, or equivalent official purpose — territory-neutral term replacing 'probate'. unknown: basis of valuation is unknown.
valuationConfidence.description: Level of confidence in the stated valuation.

condition.$comment: excellent: like new or pristine. good: normal wear, fully functional. fair: showing age, may need minor repair. poor: significant wear, may need major repair. unknown: condition has not been assessed. not_applicable: condition is not relevant (e.g. bank accounts, pensions).
condition.description: Physical condition of the asset. Use 'not_applicable' for financial assets where condition is meaningless.

quantity.description: Number of items if this represents multiple identical or similar items.

location.description: Where this asset is physically stored or held — a bank, safe, property, or institution.

propertyId.description: Reference to a Property.id if this asset is located at a specific property in the estate.

identifiers.description: External identifiers — serial numbers, account numbers, policy references, registration numbers, etc.

images.description: Photographs, videos, and document scans of this asset. Use viewType to categorise each media item for efficient browsing by executors and dealers.

possessionStatus.$comment: possessed_at_death: asset was in the deceased's possession or control at death (muchzak in halacha). receivable: asset is owed to the estate but not yet received — e.g. insurance payouts, debts owed to the deceased (ra'ui in halacha). contingent: asset depends on a future event that may or may not occur. Under halacha, the bekhor's double portion applies only to 'possessed_at_death' (muchzak), not 'receivable' (ra'ui). Useful for probate valuations universally.
possessionStatus.description: Whether the asset was held at death, is receivable post-death, or is contingent on a future event.

mobilityType.$comment: immoveable: land or buildings (rare for assets — typically use Property). moveable: personal property, financial assets, vehicles. mixed: combination. Relevant for Shia inheritance and French lex rei sitae.
mobilityType.description: Whether this asset is classified as immoveable or moveable property. Affects which conflict-of-laws rules apply in cross-border estates.

acquisitionType.$comment: self_acquired: purchased or earned by the owner. ancestral_joint: undivided coparcenary property. ancestral_severed: partitioned former joint property. inherited: received through inheritance. gifted: received as a gift. stridhan: Hindu woman's absolute property. communal: communal or clan property. waqf_endowed: Islamic endowment.
acquisitionType.description: How this asset was acquired. Determines succession rules in Indian and other customary systems.

registrationStatus.$comment: formally_registered: documented with an official authority. informally_held: possessed but not registered. community_acknowledged: community recognises ownership. disputed: ownership is contested. undocumented: no documentation exists.
registrationStatus.description: Whether ownership of this asset is formally registered or documented.

ownershipEvidence.$comment: title_deed: formal ownership document. certificate_of_occupancy: Nigerian occupancy certificate. family_recognition: family acknowledges ownership. community_testimony: community can attest. receipts_only: purchase receipts only. none: no evidence available.
ownershipEvidence.description: The type of evidence available to prove ownership of this asset.

applicableSuccessionLaw.description: The succession law governing this specific asset, if different from the general estate law.

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction whose succession law governs this asset.

beneficiaryDesignation.$comment: For assets with named beneficiaries (retirement accounts, life insurance, POD bank accounts). These pass OUTSIDE the estate.
beneficiaryDesignation.description: Named beneficiary designation for this asset. Assets with beneficiary designations pass outside the probate estate — the will does not control their distribution.

primaryBeneficiaryPersonIds.description: Person IDs of the primary beneficiaries.

contingentBeneficiaryPersonIds.description: Person IDs of contingent beneficiaries (receive if primary beneficiaries predecease).

designationType.$comment: retirement_account: 401(k), IRA, SIPP, etc. life_insurance: life insurance policy. superannuation: Australian superannuation fund. pod_account: payable-on-death bank account. other: any other type of beneficiary designation.

linkedNonprobateTransferId.description: Reference to the NonprobateTransfer entity that tracks this designation.

digitalAccessConsent.$comment: RUFADAA (Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act) — adopted by ~47 US states. Controls whether fiduciaries can access digital assets.
digitalAccessConsent.description: Digital access consent under RUFADAA or equivalent legislation. Governs whether executors and fiduciaries can access the deceased's digital accounts and assets.

consentGiven.description: Whether the account holder has given consent for fiduciary access.

scope.$comment: full_access: fiduciary may access all content and account data. limited_access: fiduciary may access only specified data (e.g. financial records but not personal messages). no_access: fiduciary may not access any content.
scope.description: The scope of access granted to fiduciaries.

designatedRecipientPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id designated to receive access to this digital asset.

onlineToolDirective.$comment: Whether the user set access directives via the platform's own tool (takes priority under RUFADAA).
onlineToolDirective.description: Whether the user set access directives through the platform's own tool (e.g. Google Inactive Account Manager, Facebook Legacy Contact). Platform directives take priority under RUFADAA.

passesOutsideEstate.$comment: True if this asset passes outside probate (beneficiary designation, POD, TOD). NOT distributed under the will.
passesOutsideEstate.description: Whether this asset passes outside the probate estate. Beneficiary designations, POD accounts, and jointly held assets typically bypass the will.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this asset. Use for provenance, sentimental value, special handling instructions, or anything not captured by structured fields.
examples: Belonged to great-grandmother — significant sentimental value to the family
Firearm requires Section 1 certificate — must be surrendered or transferred to a licence holder within 7 days of death

description.description: Structured description of the asset — what it is, its significance, and key details a dealer or executor would need. Distinct from 'notes' which is free-form.
examples: OO gauge Hornby R3456 Class 66 locomotive in EWS livery, DCC fitted, with original box and instructions
18ct yellow gold solitaire engagement ring, 1.2ct round brilliant diamond, GIA certified VS1/G

brand.description: The manufacturer or brand name. Free text — implementations should offer category-aware suggestions from the category guidance reference data, but any value is valid. Brands change over time (acquisitions, rebrands) so this is not an enum.

model.description: The specific model, product line, or range name within the brand. Combined with brand and identifiers, this uniquely describes the item to a specialist dealer.

purchaseDate.description: Date the asset was acquired by the current owner. Used for capital gains tax calculations, insurance claims, and provenance. Maps to Schema.org purchaseDate.

originalPackaging.description: Whether original packaging, documentation, and accessories are present. Affects value by 20–40% across watches, model railways, jewellery, and many other domains.
originalPackaging.$comment: complete: original box, papers/certificates, all accessories present. partial: some original packaging elements present. box_only: original box but no papers. papers_only: certificates/papers but no box. none: no original packaging. unknown: packaging status not assessed.

custodian.description: Third party currently holding this asset — a bank (safe deposit), storage facility (bonded wine), repairer (watch at jeweller), gallery (art on loan), or institution. The executor needs to know who to contact.

name.description: Name of the person or organisation holding the asset.

relationship.description: The nature of the custodial relationship.

reference.description: Account or reference number with the custodian.

conditionSystem.description: The grading system used for the conditionGrade value. Allows domain-specific condition assessment alongside the generic condition field.
conditionSystem.$comment: goldmine: Goldmine Grading Standard for vinyl records (M/NM/VG+/VG/G+/G/F/P). sheldon: Sheldon scale for coins (1-70, where 70 is perfect). ags: American Gem Society for gemstones (0-10). bsc: British Standards for antiques. gia: Gemological Institute of America for diamonds.

conditionGrade.description: The specific grade within the conditionSystem. Meaningful only when conditionSystem is set.

urgency.$comment: immediate: must be dealt with at once — live animals, perishable goods, firearms (7-day surrender in UK). within_7_days: urgent but not immediate — vehicles with insurance expiring, time-limited subscriptions. within_30_days: should be addressed within a month — tenanted property, business interests needing continuity. none: no time pressure — most personal possessions, financial accounts.
urgency.description: How urgently this asset needs attention after the testator's death. Drives the executor's priority list.

urgencyReason.description: Explanation of why this asset is urgent. Particularly important for 'immediate' items.
examples: Firearm requires Section 1 certificate — must be surrendered or transferred to a licence holder within 7 days of death
Perishable wine collection in temperature-controlled storage — power must be maintained

containedInAssetId.description: Reference to another Asset.id if this asset is physically contained within or logically part of another asset. Enables hierarchical asset nesting — e.g. a watch inside a safe, tools inside a toolbox.

insurance.description: Insurance coverage for this asset. The executor needs to know what's insured, by whom, and whether cover continues after death.

insuredValue.description: The insured value in minor currency units.

renewalDate.description: Next renewal date. Important — cover may lapse if not renewed after death.

examples: Cover continues for 30 days after death per policy terms

purchasedFrom.description: Where the asset was purchased or acquired from. Collectors track provenance — valuable for dealers and for establishing authenticity.

importSourceId.description: Reference to an importSources entry on the root document, indicating which system this entity was imported from.

dataProvenance.$comment: Per-entity override of the document-level dataProvenance. Same values as the root field.
dataProvenance.description: How this specific entity's data was captured. Overrides the document-level default if set.

searchTerms.description: Keywords for marketplace search — AI-generated or human-curated. Memoization: once derived, cached here so subsequent agents reuse them instantly rather than re-deriving.

comparableSearchProfile.description: Structured search instructions for comparable-finding agents. Acts as agent memory — the item teaches future agents how to find its peers.

searchQuery.description: Natural-language query an agent should use.
examples: Hornby R3456 Class 66 OO gauge boxed

filters.description: Key-value filters to narrow results. Agents map to platform-specific parameters.

lastSearchedAt.description: When an agent last executed this search profile.

searchFrequency.$comment: once: search once and cache. weekly: re-search weekly for price trends. monthly: monthly refresh. on_demand: only when explicitly triggered.
searchFrequency.description: How often this item should be re-searched.

suggestedSubcategory.description: AI-suggested subcategory refinement, kept separate from the human-set subcategory. Accepted when the user confirms. Provenance: always AI-generated.

shareholding.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for shares and shareholdings. Use when category is 'shares' or 'investment'.
shareholding.description: Detailed shareholding information — company, share class, voting rights, restrictions. Relevant for BPR (UK), stepped-up basis (US), and controlling interest calculations.

companyName.description: Name of the company in which shares are held.

totalSharesIssued.description: Total shares issued by the company (for calculating percentage ownership).

votingRights.description: Whether these shares carry voting rights.

restrictedTransfer.description: Whether transfer of these shares is restricted (e.g. pre-emption rights, shareholder agreement).

listedExchange.description: Stock exchange where shares are listed, if publicly traded. Null/absent for private companies.

businessInterest.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for business interests. Use when category is 'business_interest'.
businessInterest.description: Detailed business interest information — partnership shares, LLP membership, sole trader business. Relevant for BPR (UK), QFOBI (US), and succession planning.

businessType.$comment: sole_trader: individual trading. partnership: traditional partnership. llp: limited liability partnership. limited_company: private limited company (Ltd). plc: public limited company. franchise: franchise operation. cooperative: cooperative business. other: any other structure.

controllingInterest.description: Whether the testator holds a controlling interest. Affects BPR eligibility and valuation discounts.

partnershipAgreement.description: Whether a written partnership/operating agreement exists.

successionProvision.description: What the partnership/operating agreement says about succession on death.
examples: Surviving partners must buy out deceased's share at book value
Option to purchase at market value within 90 days

annualTurnover.description: Approximate annual turnover — helps with BPR qualification and valuation.

pension.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for pension and retirement accounts. Use when category is 'pension'.
pension.description: Detailed pension information — scheme type, nominated beneficiaries, death benefits. Pensions often pass outside the estate via nomination.

schemeType.$comment: defined_benefit: employer guarantees a specific retirement income. defined_contribution: value depends on contributions and investment returns. sipp: self-invested personal pension (UK). state_pension: government state pension. annuity: purchased annuity providing guaranteed income. drawdown: flexible drawdown from pension pot. other: any other pension type.

nominatedBeneficiaries.description: Person IDs of nominated beneficiaries. Pension trustees usually have discretion but will consider nominations.

nominationDate.description: Date the beneficiary nomination was last updated.

deathBenefitType.$comment: lump_sum: a one-off payment to beneficiaries. dependants_pension: ongoing pension payments to dependants. drawdown_transfer: pension pot transfers to beneficiary for drawdown. annuity_guarantee: guaranteed payments for remaining guarantee period. none: no death benefit (e.g. annuity with no guarantee).
deathBenefitType.description: Type of death benefit payable from this pension.

crystallised.description: Whether the pension has been accessed/crystallised. Affects tax treatment on death (UK: pre-75 tax-free, post-75 taxed as income).

lifetimeAllowanceUsed.description: Percentage of lifetime allowance used (UK). Abolished April 2024 but transitional protections remain.

insurancePolicy.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for life insurance and endowment policies. Use when category is 'insurance'.
insurancePolicy.description: Life insurance policy details — type, sum assured, beneficiaries, trust status. Policies written in trust pass outside the estate.

policyType.$comment: term_life: pays out on death within term. whole_life: pays out whenever death occurs. endowment: savings + life cover, matures at set date. critical_illness: pays on diagnosis of specified illness. income_protection: replaces income during illness. key_person: business policy on key employee. other: any other type.

sumAssured.description: The amount payable on death, in minor currency units.

writtenInTrust.description: Whether the policy is written in trust. If true, proceeds pass outside the estate and are not subject to inheritance tax.

trusteeNames.description: Names of the policy trustees, if written in trust.

coOwnership.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for co-owned assets. Use when the asset has shared ownership that affects how it passes on death.
coOwnership.description: Co-ownership details for assets held jointly or in common with others. Determines whether the asset passes by survivorship or under the will.

ownershipType.$comment: joint_tenants: right of survivorship — passes automatically to survivor(s). tenants_in_common: no survivorship — each owner's share passes under their will. community_property: marital community property (US). partnership: business partnership asset. other: other arrangement.
ownershipType.description: How ownership is structured between co-owners.

severanceDate.description: Date joint tenancy was severed to become tenants in common (if applicable).

intellectualProperty.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for IP assets. Use when category is 'intellectual_property'.
intellectualProperty.description: Intellectual property details — patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets. IP succession varies by jurisdiction and type.

ipType.$comment: patent: registered invention. copyright: creative works (literary, artistic, musical). trademark: registered brand/logo. design_right: registered or unregistered design. trade_secret: confidential business information. database_right: sui generis database right (EU/UK). other: any other IP type.

annualRevenue.description: Approximate annual revenue generated from this IP (licensing, royalties, etc.).

stockCompensation.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for employee stock options, RSUs, and equity compensation. Common in tech sector estates.
stockCompensation.description: Employee stock compensation details — options, RSUs, ESPPs. Vesting schedules and exercise windows are critical on death.

compensationType.$comment: iso: incentive stock option (US tax-advantaged). nso: non-qualified stock option. rsu: restricted stock unit. espp: employee stock purchase plan. phantom_stock: phantom equity (cash settlement). sar: stock appreciation right. other: any other type.

unvestedQuantity.description: Number of shares/units not yet vested. May be forfeited on death depending on plan terms.

exercisePrice.description: Strike/exercise price per share for options.

expirationDate.description: Date the options expire. Estate typically has limited window to exercise after death.

postDeathExerciseWindow.description: Time allowed for estate to exercise options after death.

acceleratesOnDeath.description: Whether unvested awards accelerate (fully vest) on death.

debtReceivable.$comment: Category-specific sub-object for debts owed TO the estate. The mirror of a liability — someone else owes the testator money.
debtReceivable.description: Details of money owed to the testator by others. These are estate assets that the executor must collect.

debtorName.description: Name of the person or organisation that owes the debt.

debtorPersonId.description: Reference to Person.id if the debtor is in the people array.

interestRate.description: Annual interest rate on the debt, as a percentage.

secured.description: Whether the debt is secured against collateral.

documentRef.description: Reference to any loan agreement or promissory note.

collectibility.$comment: likely: debtor is solvent and able to pay. uncertain: some risk of non-payment. doubtful: significant risk of non-payment. uncollectible: debt is effectively worthless.
